content.text: vscode_state_sync outputs each and every file to INFO. that is not in line with convention that file-level info is sent to FULL. Probably just a "syncing vscode state db's" and "<n> files synced" is enough on INFO.
I'll look at the logging in vscode_state_sync.

246	    async def execute(self) -> None:
247	        """Selectively merge each present editor state DB (main + ``.backup``) onto the target.
248	
249	        Iterates ``VSCODE_STATE_HANDLED_RELPATHS`` — every file folder_sync excludes, so
250	        the exclude set and the merge set are identical (no exclude without merge). Skips
251	        files absent on the source, and for each present one runs source-strip -> transfer
252	        -> target-inject -> atomic ``mv`` (Step C skipped when the target file is absent —
253	        first sync). In dry-run the job only detects source/target presence and logs the
254	        intended actions, performing no ``send_file``, no target inject, and no ``mv``
255	        (ADR-014).
256	        """
257	        # A DB's absolute path is identical on source and target: the invoking (real)
258	        # user has the same uid and home path on every machine, and pc-switcher does no
259	        # user/path mapping (ADR-019). So no target-home remapping here.
260	        home = Path.home()
261	        prefix = "[dry-run] " if self.context.dry_run else ""
262	        globs = PRESERVE_KEY_GLOBS
263	
264	        present = [rel for rel in VSCODE_STATE_HANDLED_RELPATHS if (home / rel).exists()]
265	        if not present:
266	            self._log(Host.SOURCE, LogLevel.INFO, f"{prefix}No VS Code state DBs found on source; nothing to sync")
267	            self._report_progress(ProgressUpdate(percent=100))
268	            return
269	
270	        total = len(present)
271	        for index, relpath in enumerate(present):
272	            source_db = home / relpath
273	            target_db = (home / relpath).as_posix()
274	            label = db_label(relpath)
275	
276	            target_exists = (
277	                await self.target.run_command(f"test -f {shlex.quote(target_db)}", login_shell=False)
278	            ).success
279	            mode = "merge" if target_exists else "first-sync"
280	
281	            if self.context.dry_run:
282	                self._log(
283	                    Host.TARGET,
284	                    LogLevel.INFO,
285	                    f"{prefix}Would sync {label} ({mode}); preserving keys matching {globs}",
286	                )
287	                self._report_progress(ProgressUpdate(percent=int((index + 1) / total * 100)))
288	                continue
289	
290	            await self._sync_editor(source_db, target_db, target_exists, globs, label)
291	            self._report_progress(ProgressUpdate(percent=int((index + 1) / total * 100)))
292	
293	        self._report_progress(ProgressUpdate(percent=100))
294	
295	    async def _sync_editor(
296	        self,
297	        source_db: Path,
298	        target_db: str,
299	        target_exists: bool,
300	        globs: Sequence[str],
301	        label: str,
302	    ) -> None:
303	        """Run the source-strip -> transfer -> inject -> atomic-mv sequence for one DB file.
304	
305	        Handles either a main ``state.vscdb`` or its ``.backup`` sidecar (same schema);
306	        ``label`` names which. The neutral DB is transferred to
307	        ``<target_db>.pcswitcher-tmp`` — same directory as the live file — so the final
308	        ``mv -f`` is atomic and preserves ownership/perms.
309	        """
310	        remote_tmp = target_db + ".pcswitcher-tmp"
311	        tmp_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="pcswitcher-vscode-"))
312	        local_tmp = tmp_dir / "state.vscdb"
313	        try:
314	            # Step A — source-strip: copy the live source DB, then delete preserved rows.
315	            # In-process: the copy is source-local, so no subprocess or shell is involved.
316	            shutil.copyfile(source_db, local_tmp)
317	            try:
318	                _run_sql(local_tmp, source_strip_sql(globs))
319	            except sqlite3.Error as error:
320	                self._raise(Host.SOURCE, label, "source-strip", str(error))
321	
322	            # Step B — transfer the neutral DB into the target live DB's directory.
323	            # Ensure that directory exists first: folder_sync normally creates it, but
324	            # jobs are independently toggleable, so `folder_sync: false` + this job on a
325	            # target that never ran the editor would otherwise leave the SFTP put with no
326	            # parent directory. mkdir -p is a no-op when it already exists.
327	            mkdir = await self.target.run_command(
328	                f"mkdir -p {shlex.quote(Path(target_db).parent.as_posix())}", login_shell=False
329	            )
330	            if not mkdir.success:
331	                self._raise(Host.TARGET, label, "mkdir target dir", mkdir.stderr)
332	            await self.target.send_file(local_tmp, remote_tmp)
333	
334	            # Step C — target-inject the target's own preserved rows, only when it has a live DB.
335	            if target_exists:
336	                inject = await self.target.run_command(
337	                    target_sql_command(remote_tmp, target_inject_sql(target_db, globs)),
338	                    login_shell=False,
339	                )
340	                if not inject.success:
341	                    self._raise(Host.TARGET, label, "target-inject", inject.stderr)
342	
343	            # Atomic replace within the same directory.
344	            move = await self.target.run_command(
345	                f"mv -f {shlex.quote(remote_tmp)} {shlex.quote(target_db)}", login_shell=False
346	            )
347	            if not move.success:
348	                self._raise(Host.TARGET, label, "atomic mv", move.stderr)
349	
350	            self._log(
351	                Host.TARGET,
352	                LogLevel.INFO,
353	                f"Synced {label} ({'merge' if target_exists else 'first-sync'})",
354	            )
355	        finally:
356	            shutil.rmtree(tmp_dir, ignore_errors=True)
357	
358	    def _raise(self, host: Host, label: str, step: str, stderr: str) -> None:
359	        """Log CRITICAL and raise; any exception halts the sync (orchestrator contract)."""
360	        message = f"{label} {step} failed: {stderr.strip()}"
361	        self._log(host, LogLevel.CRITICAL, message)
362	        raise RuntimeError(message)

content.text:
INFO now carries only job-level lines; per-file detail moved to FULL.

- `execute()`: one INFO `"Syncing VS Code state DBs"` at start, one INFO `"<n> VS Code state DB file(s) synced"` at end (both dry-run-prefixed).
- Per-file `"Would sync <label> (...)"` and `"Synced <label> (...)"` → FULL.
- The "no DBs found" early-return stays INFO.

Lint, types, and the 33 vscode unit tests pass.
make pr (to have a trace) and merge that pr immediately when unit and lint tests passed. Don't wait for integration tests.

input.command:
git checkout -b fix/vscode-state-sync-log-levels && git add -A && git commit -q -F - <<'EOF' && git push -u origin HEAD 2>&1 | tail -3
fix(vscode_state_sync): log per-file detail at FULL, not INFO

INFO now carries only the job-level start line and the final synced count;
per-file "Synced <db>" / dry-run "Would sync <db>" messages move to FULL,
matching the file-level-detail-at-FULL convention used by folder_sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/REDACTED
EOF
